meta.name: React + Vite + Electron (Desktop App)
meta.description: Production-ready Electron desktop application with React 19, Vite, Zustand state management. Manages filesystem operations, GitHub API integration, and SSH key management via secure IPC bridge.

architecture.summary: A secure desktop application architecture using Electron with a React+Vite renderer. The main process handles privileged operations (filesystem, GitHub API, SSH key management) and exposes them to the renderer via a typed IPC bridge. Context isolation and preload scripts enforce a strict security boundary between the UI and system access.

layers.name: UI Layer (CMP)
layers.description: React components rendered in the Electron BrowserWindow. Purely presentational — no Node.js APIs, no direct IPC calls. Receives data from stores via hooks.
rules: State comes from STO via hooks

layers.name: State Layer (STO)
layers.description: Zustand stores managing UI and domain state. Stores call the IPC bridge (exposed via window.api) for privileged operations. Single source of truth for renderer state.
rules: Never imports electron or Node.js modules

layers.name: IPC Bridge (BRG)
layers.description: Preload script that selectively exposes main-process capabilities to the renderer via contextBridge. Defines a typed, minimal API surface. All calls are async and validated.

layers.name: Main Process Services (SVC)
layers.description: Node.js services running in the Electron main process. Handle filesystem operations, GitHub API calls, SSH key management, and other privileged system access.

layers.name: Adapters (ADP)
layers.description: Low-level adapters wrapping Node.js APIs and external services. fs adapter for filesystem, Octokit for GitHub, ssh2/keygen for SSH.

architecture.dataFlow: CMP -> STO -> window.api (BRG) -> IPC -> SVC (main) -> ADP -> System/Network
keyPrinciples: Context isolation enabled — renderer cannot access Node.js APIs directly
Preload script is the ONLY bridge between renderer and main process
All IPC channels are explicitly whitelisted and typed
Filesystem access scoped to user-approved directories
GitHub tokens stored in OS keychain via safeStorage, never in plaintext
SSH private keys never leave main process — only public keys and metadata sent to renderer
Vite HMR in dev; Electron Forge/Builder for production packaging
architecture.securityModel: Context isolation + sandbox enabled. Preload exposes a minimal typed API via contextBridge. No nodeIntegration in renderer. All filesystem paths validated and sandboxed. Secrets stored via Electron safeStorage. SSH operations never expose private key material to the renderer.
architecture.buildProcess: Vite builds the React app to dist/renderer. Electron main process code is compiled separately. electron-builder or electron-forge packages everything into platform-specific installers (dmg, AppImage, nsis).

shared._note: Types and constants shared between main and renderer

files.client/package.json:
{
  "name": "electron-react-app",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "main": "dist/main/index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "concurrently -k \"vite\" \"electron .\"",
    "dev:vite": "vite",
    "dev:electron": "tsc -p tsconfig.main.json && electron .",
    "build": "tsc -p tsconfig.main.json && vite build",
    "build:main": "tsc -p tsconfig.main.json",
    "build:renderer": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "lint": "eslint src/",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "package": "npm run build && electron-builder",
    "package:mac": "npm run build && electron-builder --mac",
    "package:linux": "npm run build && electron-builder --linux",
    "package:win": "npm run build && electron-builder --win"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "react-router": "^7.0.0",
    "zustand": "^5.0.0",
    "@octokit/rest": "^21.0.0",
    "electron-store": "^10.0.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.0",
    "tailwind-merge": "^3.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "electron": "^35.0.0",
    "electron-builder": "^25.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.4.0",
    "vite": "^6.0.0",
    "vite-plugin-electron": "^0.28.0",
    "vite-plugin-electron-renderer": "^0.14.0",
    "concurrently": "^9.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0",
    "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.0.0",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.0",
    "postcss": "^8.5.0"
  }
}
files.client/tsconfig.json:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/renderer/*"],
      "@shared/*": ["./src/shared/*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["src/renderer/**/*", "src/shared/**/*", "src/preload/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
files.client/electron-builder.yml:
appId: com.app.electron-react
productName: Electron React App
directories:
  output: release
  buildResources: resources
files:
  - dist/**/*
  - "!node_modules/**/*"
mac:
  target:
    - dmg
    - zip
  category: public.app-category.developer-tools
linux:
  target:
    - AppImage
    - deb
  category: Development
win:
  target:
    - nsis
    - zip
nsis:
  oneClick: false
  allowToChangeInstallationDirectory: true

CMP.description: React component (UI only, no Node.js or IPC)

STO.description: Zustand store (state + window.api calls for system access)

ADP.description: Adapter wrapping a Node.js or external API (fs, Octokit, ssh)

FNC.description: Shared utility function (pure, no side effects)

notes: Dev mode runs Vite + Electron concurrently with HMR
Production: 'npm run package' builds platform-specific installer
GitHub token stored via Electron safeStorage (OS keychain)
SSH operations use system ssh-keygen and ssh-add — no extra deps
Context isolation is ON — renderer cannot access Node.js APIs
All IPC channels defined in src/shared/ipc-channels.ts
Filesystem operations sandboxed to user's home directory
